text:
Former University of Michigan swimmer Siobhán Haughey is an Olympic medalist once again.

The 26-year-old from Hong Kong claimed bronze in the 200-meter freestyle on Monday in Paris. She posted a time of 1:54.55, trailing only Australia’s Mollie O’Callaghan (1:53.27) and Ariarne Titmus (1:53.81).

Bronze for Siobhan‼️🥉#OlympiansMadeHere#MGoParis#GoBluepic.twitter.com/3zVXylLYYu

Haughey, who is competing in her third Olympics, entered the final heat with the fourth-fastest entry time (1:55.51) in the event. She won silver in the 100- and 200-meter freestyle in Tokyo, becoming the first Wolverines women’s swimmer to win two individual medals in the same Olympic games.

She also competed in the 2016 Games in Rio before her sophomore year at Michigan.

Haughey, a 15-time Big Ten champion at Michigan from 2015-19, will get a chance at another medal in Paris as well. She will compete in the 100 freestyle, with heats and semifinals slated for Tuesday. The final is Wednesday.

She is one of 15 swimmers with UM ties competing in Paris. Defending 100-meter butterfly gold medalist Maggie MacNeil (Canada) narrowly missed the podium Sunday, placing fifth in the same event.

RELATED:Michigan duo helps USA men’s gymnastics end Olympic medal drought

Ryan Zuke is a sports reporter for MLive.com and covers University of Michigan athletics. He writes about a variety of sports with a primary focus on football and recruiting. With the success of many Wolverines...more
